name: Parque Nacional de Monfragüe — circular desde Monfragüe
region_intro: Touring cycling around Malpartida De Plasencia offers routes through the diverse landscapes of the Cáceres province, serving as a gateway to the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve and National Park. The region is characterized by extensive *dehesas* of holm and cork oaks, alongside river valleys and reservoirs. Many routes utilize former railway lines, known as Vías Verdes, providing accessible paths through rural scenery. The terrain varies from mostly paved surfaces suitable for all skill levels to more challenging routes with significant elevation gains within the National Park. Best touring cycling routes around Malpartida De Plasencia The most popular touring cycling route is Parque Nacional de Monfragüe — circular desde Monfragüe, a 45.5 miles (73.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 53 minutes to complete. This route explores the varied landscapes and wildlife habitats of Monfragüe National Park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Monfragüe Greenway – Bazagona Greenway loop from Monfragüe, a moderate 22.6 miles (36.4 km) path. This route follows a Vía Verde, offering a journey through rural landscapes dotted with holm oaks. Local touring cyclists also love the Torrejón-Tiétar Dam – La Higuerilla Viewpoint loop from Monfragüe, a 37.0 miles (59.6 km) trail leading through the Monfragüe National Park and skirting reservoirs, often completed in about 3 hours 43 minutes. Touring cycling around Malpartida De Plasencia is defined by the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve, picturesque *dehesas*, and repurposed Vías Verdes. The network offers options for easy rides on greenways as well as more challenging routes with notable elevation changes. The routes in Malpartida De Plasencia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Malpartida De Plasencia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Malpartida De Plasencia?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Malpartida De Plasencia, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult trails to explore.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Malpartida De Plasencia?
A: The terrain varies significantly. Many routes utilize former railway lines, known as Vías Verdes, offering mostly paved surfaces suitable for all skill levels. Other routes traverse picturesque *dehesas* (traditional agroforestry systems with holm and cork oaks) and can include more challenging sections with notable elevation gains, especially within the Monfragüe National Park.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes. The Vías Verdes (Greenways) are particularly suitable for beginners and families due to their generally flat and paved surfaces. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31432301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bazagona Greenway – Monfragüe Greenway loop from Monfragüe&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that follows a greenway, offering a pleasant journey through rural landscapes.

Q: Which routes offer the best opportunities for wildlife spotting, especially birds?
A: Routes within or bordering the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve and National Park are excellent for wildlife spotting. This area is a globally recognized birdwatching hotspot. You might spot species like the Spanish imperial eagle, griffon vulture, and eagle owl. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19433533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Majalon Road – Chinata Ascent loop from Monfragüe&lt;/a&gt; offers a chance to explore the natural environment of the park.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Malpartida De Plasencia?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19433533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Majalon Road – Chinata Ascent loop from Monfragüe&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate circular route that takes you through varied landscapes.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can visit stunning viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Higuerilla Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serrano Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5679306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Gypsy's Leap&lt;/a&gt; (Salto del Gitano) and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2858001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Falcón&lt;/a&gt; are magnificent rock formations within Monfragüe National Park. You'll also find routes skirting reservoirs such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5391554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Plasencia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I explore near the cycling trails?
A: Beyond the natural landscapes, you can explore historical sites. The 16th-century Church of San Juan Bautista in Malpartida de Plasencia is an Asset of Cultural Interest. Nearby, Cerro de los Castillejos features remains of a Vetton settlement, ancient menhirs, and anthropomorphic tombs. Monfragüe Castle, with its Muslim origins, also offers historical insights and panoramic views.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Malpartida De Plasencia?
A: The touring cycling routes around Malpartida De Plasencia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. Over 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the diverse landscapes and well-maintained paths.

Q: Is it possible to access the cycling routes using public transport?
A: While Malpartida de Plasencia is a gateway to the Monfragüe National Park, specific public transport options directly to trailheads can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to a starting point, especially for routes deeper within the park. The Vías Verdes often have more accessible entry points.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, the region offers options for longer rides. While specific multi-day routes aren't detailed here, the extensive network of trails, including those within Monfragüe National Park and the Vías Verdes, can be combined to create longer touring experiences. The Tajo Natural Trail also winds through the National Park, suitable for touring.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Malpartida De Plasencia?
A: Spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) are generally the best times for touring cycling. The weather is milder, and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable, while winters are generally mild but can be damp.

Q: Are there any cycling routes that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, several routes offer views of or pass by local water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6910612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrejón-Tiétar Dam – La Higuerilla Viewpoint loop from Monfragüe&lt;/a&gt;, for example, leads through Monfragüe National Park and skirts reservoirs like Embalse de las Covachillas and Pantano del Horco del Espino, offering scenic waterside stretches.
tips:
Torrejón el Rubio is an excellent destination for those seeking unspoiled nature, wildlife viewing, hiking, and peaceful tourism surrounded by the privileged environment of Monfragüe National Park, one of the best places in Spain for spotting birds such as the black vulture and the Spanish imperial eagle.

Furthermore, its historical heritage, the friendliness of the locals, and its strategic location make it a very complete place for a weekend getaway or as a base for exploring Monfragüe.

Nice country hotel. It was very quiet in March. Very good kitchen (evenings). Breakfast is standard, you have a nice view outside through panoramic windows. It is 9 km to the highlight of the Monfragüe National Park, the rock with the many griffon vultures (Salto Del Gitano; bring binoculars!), but also to the Castillo de Monfragüe Castle with a fantastic 360-degree panoramic view (and rock paintings accessible by entrance fee). It goes downhill in serpentines for almost 100 m. Near the Hospederia there is also a bridge of Roman origin within walking distance.
Here you can watch the vultures very well. Definitely take a good pair of binoculars with you. It's fascinating when the vultures glide through the air using the thermals.
This viewpoint is ideal for birdwatching, especially birds of prey and cliff-dwelling birds. It's also surrounded by lush vegetation.
